reusable_components.description: EXIST in codebase. Can be reused.

items.name: page_title
items.constant_value: HP Instant Ink – Ink Monthly Subscription | HP Official Site

items.name: execute_verify_action
items.what_it_does: Executes AI-powered visual verification of the current page state. Extracts verification parameters from step, captures screenshot, constructs prompt with variable substitution, calls run_ai_task() with reference image (optional) and test screenshot to perform AI vision verification using Azure OpenAI. Returns verification result.
items.method_signature: def execute_verify_action(self, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:

items.name: run_ai_task
items.what_it_does: Performs AI-powered visual verification using Azure OpenAI vision models. Extracts Azure configuration, encodes reference and test images to base64, constructs messages for API call with prompt and images, calls Azure OpenAI chat completions API with GPT-4 Vision, handles streaming or non-streaming responses, parses JSON result from AI response. Returns parsed verification result as dictionary with status and details.

items.name: execute_click_action
items.what_it_does: Executes a click action on a UI element based on step parameters. Extracts element_id, label, page, text, expand, parent, and multiple from step. Loads JSON metadata for specified page if provided. Handles expand logic to check if element is already expanded. Calls get_control() to find control by text or label. Handles iframe switching if control has iframe definition. Calls click_control() with progressive fallback strategies to click the element.
items.method_signature: def execute_click_action(self, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:

items.name: execute_vscroll_action
items.what_it_does: Executes a vertical scroll action either to a specific element or by page. If text is specified, finds the control using get_control(), builds locator strategies, waits for element, and scrolls it into view using JavaScript scrollIntoView. If text is not specified, scrolls by page using Keys.PAGE_UP or Keys.PAGE_DOWN based on direction parameter.
items.method_signature: def execute_vscroll_action(self, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:

items.name: execute_refresh_action
items.what_it_does: Executes a browser page refresh action by calling self.driver.refresh() to reload the current page. Logs the refresh action and calls log_action() for tracking.
items.method_signature: def execute_refresh_action(self, step: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:

items.name: substitute_variables
items.what_it_does: Performs variable substitution in text strings, replacing ${VARIABLE_NAME} placeholders with actual values from the variables dictionary. Supports both simple variables (${URL}) and nested dictionary access (${PRINTER.serial_number}). Uses regex to find all ${...} patterns and replaces them with values from self.variables.

items.name: set_variables
items.what_it_does: Sets multiple variables in the executor's variables dictionary for later substitution in test steps. Iterates through all keyword arguments, logging each key-value pair, and updates self.variables dictionary. Returns True. Variables can be referenced in YAML test steps using ${VARIABLE_NAME} syntax.

items.name: take_screenshot
items.what_it_does: Captures a screenshot of the current browser page and saves it to the specified filename by calling self.driver.save_screenshot(). Returns the filename string. Used during verify actions to capture page state for AI vision analysis.

items.name: execute_flow
items.what_it_does: Executes a complete test flow from YAML data and generates a CSV result report. Validates yaml_data contains 'flow' key, extracts steps, iterates through each step calling execute_step(), records start time and duration for each step, handles exceptions, appends results to workflow_results list with step_name, start_time, duration_seconds, and status. After all steps, creates pandas DataFrame and saves to CSV if flow_result path provided. Calls print_execution_summary() at end.
items.method_signature: def execute_flow(self, yaml_data: Dict[str, Any], flow_result=None) -> None:

items.name: load_url_in_browser
items.what_it_does: Loads a URL in the specified browser, either in the current tab or a new tab. Checks if browser exists in registry. If new_tab is True, executes JavaScript window.open() to open URL in new tab, switches to new tab, and updates current_tab_index. If new_tab is False, loads URL in current tab using driver.get(). After loading, maximizes window and refreshes page. Returns True on success, False on failure.
items.method_signature: def load_url_in_browser(self, browser_name: str, url: str, new_tab: bool = False):

items.name: switch_to_browser
items.what_it_does: Switches the active browser context to the specified browser. Checks if browser_name exists in browsers registry. If bring_to_focus is True, calls bring_to_foreground() to make browser window visible and active. Sets self.current_browser to browser_name. Returns the driver instance for the specified browser.
items.method_signature: def switch_to_browser(self, browser_name: str, bring_to_focus: bool = True):

items.name: get_current_driver
items.what_it_does: Returns the Selenium WebDriver instance for the currently active browser. Checks if self.current_browser is set, and if so returns the 'driver' value from the browsers dictionary for that browser. If no browser is currently active, returns None.

items.name: YamlStepExecutor
items.what_it_does: Core test execution engine that interprets and executes YAML-based test definitions. Loads JSON metadata files containing UI element definitions, manages test variables with substitution support, executes various action types (click, set, verify, navigate, scroll, etc.), handles iframe switching, performs AI-powered visual verification using Azure OpenAI vision models, manages printer selection workflows, tracks execution logs, and generates CSV result reports. Supports multiple metadata sources (auth_controls, hpsmart, instantink, oss_emulator) and provides comprehensive error handling and logging.

items.name: MultiBrowserManager
items.what_it_does: Manages multiple browser instances with support for remote debugging, incognito mode, and normal browser sessions. Provides comprehensive window management including switching between browsers, finding windows by URL, waiting for new windows, bringing windows to foreground, and handling cross-platform window focus operations. Maintains a registry of all managed browsers with their metadata and provides utilities for listing, switching, and closing browsers.

items.name: setup
items.what_it_does: Performs complete Playwright browser setup and returns a ready-to-use Page object. Starts Playwright, determines browser type based on target platform, launches browser with configured options, creates browser context with locale/viewport/logging/video settings, creates a new page, and returns the page object.

items.name: close
items.what_it_does: Performs complete cleanup of all Playwright resources in the correct order: page, context, browser, and playwright instance. Verifies HAR and video files were saved if logging is enabled. Catches and logs exceptions during cleanup to prevent cleanup failures from crashing the test.

items.name: save_full_page_screenshot
items.what_it_does: Captures a full-page screenshot. This is imported and used by scraper.py for baseline creation, but the actual implementation is in core.utils.

items.name: PlaywrightManager
items.what_it_does: Complete browser lifecycle manager that handles browser, context, and page setup and teardown. Implements context manager protocol for clean resource management.

items.name: get_current_timestamp
items.what_it_does: Generates and returns the current timestamp as a formatted string in the pattern YYYY_MM_DD_HH_MM_SS. Used for naming output files and run identifiers.

items.name: sanitize_filename
items.what_it_does: Sanitizes a filename string by replacing all invalid filesystem characters with underscores. Returns the sanitized filename string safe for use in file operations.

items.name: take_element_screenshot
items.what_it_does: Captures a screenshot of a specific element on the page identified by a selector and saves it to the specified path. Returns True on success, False on failure.
items.method_signature: def take_element_screenshot(page: Page, selector: str, output_path: str) -> bool

items.name: freeze_scroll
items.what_it_does: Disables scrolling on a web page by injecting CSS that sets overflow to hidden. Useful when capturing screenshots to prevent the page from scrolling during automation.

items.name: unfreeze_scroll
items.what_it_does: Re-enables scrolling on a web page by injecting CSS that sets overflow to auto. Restores normal scrolling behavior after it was disabled.

items.name: extract_text
items.what_it_does: Extracts the visible inner text from a Playwright element and returns it as a trimmed string. Respects CSS visibility and display properties.

items.name: is_element_hidden_or_disabled
items.what_it_does: Checks if a Playwright element is hidden or disabled by evaluating JavaScript. Returns True if element is hidden via CSS or disabled.

items.name: get_element_position
items.what_it_does: Retrieves the position and dimensions of a Playwright element as a dictionary with x, y, width, and height keys.

items.name: extract_styles
items.what_it_does: Extracts computed CSS styles from a Playwright element including font_family, font_size, font_weight, color, background, and text_align properties.

items.name: validate_context
items.what_it_does: Main validation function that compares live page state against baseline scraped data with comprehensive element, text, screenshot, and animation validation. Supports screenshot-only mode for quick checkpoints.
items.method_signature: def validate_context(flow_name, context_name, page, excel_path, output_folder, validation_workbook_path=None, screenshot_only=False, sub_context=None, lang_code=None, animations=False, target_dir=None)

items.name: compare_images_detect_then_filter
items.what_it_does: Performs pixel-by-pixel image comparison with exclusion region filtering and bounding box generation for failures. Returns (is_pass, diff, boxes, failed_pixels) tuple.

items.name: DashboardHelper
items.what_it_does: Comprehensive static helper class providing utility methods for all dashboard-related test operations including dashboard access, navigation, first-time setup with modal handling, and various dashboard UI interactions.

items.name: CancellationPlanHelper
items.what_it_does: Static helper class providing utility methods for testing subscription cancellation flows. While primarily focused on cancellation, it may contain methods for pause plan functionality which is related to this test case.

items.name: start_enrollment_and_sign_in
items.what_it_does: Starts enrollment and signs in the user with retry logic to handle timing issues. Navigates to enrollment URL, accepts privacy banner, and performs HPID authentication. Includes retry logic with configurable timeout to handle intermittent failures.

items.name: subscription_to_subscribed
items.what_it_does: Transitions a subscription to 'subscribed' state if not already in that state via Gemini Rails Admin. Checks current state, accesses edit menu, selects state transition event, saves changes, and verifies the state is now 'subscribed'.

items.name: verify_rails_admin_info
items.what_it_does: Verifies that a Rails Admin field has the expected value with optional retry logic. Gets field text by title, asserts it equals expected value, includes retry with page reloads to handle async updates. Allows verification to wait up to 2 minutes for async updates to propagate.

items.name: event_shift
items.what_it_does: Shifts subscription events forward in time by a specified number of days, optionally forcing billing cycle creation. Accesses edit menu, fills rollback field with days to shift, optionally enables billing cycle creation checkbox, saves changes, and verifies success. Commonly used to simulate time passing in tests without waiting for real time.

items.name: EnrollmentHelper
items.what_it_does: Comprehensive helper class providing static utility methods for automating HP Instant Ink enrollment flows. Contains over 100 methods covering all enrollment stages including terms acceptance, printer selection, plan selection, shipping address management, billing information, promotional codes, and enrollment completion. Supports multiple enrollment variants.

items.name: GeminiRAHelper
items.what_it_does: Comprehensive helper class providing static utility methods for interacting with Gemini Rails Admin interface. Contains over 60 methods covering tenant management, subscription operations (state transitions, free months management), billing cycle operations, payment credential management, printer operations, event shifting for time-based testing, and extensive validation methods.

items.name: EmailHelper
items.what_it_does: Helper module for email-related operations in test automation. Provides utility methods for checking email receipt, extracting email content, and validating email subjects using Gmail API integration. Used for verifying email notifications sent during test flows.

items.name: verify_previous_billing_cycle_period
items.what_it_does: Verifies that selecting a previous billing cycle changes the period title to not contain 'Current'. Clicks billing cycle dropdown, selects first previous option, and verifies the title changed.

items.name: get_billing_cycle_option
items.what_it_does: Selects a specific billing cycle period by index from the dropdown. Opens dropdown, clicks the appropriate option (1, 2, or 3), and verifies the table updated.

items.name: verify_billing_table_data
items.what_it_does: Verifies that the billing table displays the expected total pages printed. Extracts numbers from the total pages printed element and validates against expected value.

items.name: verify_correct_billing_section
items.what_it_does: Verifies all billing section fields against expected values from a dictionary. Validates total pages printed, plan pages, rollover pages, additional pages, plan price, overage price, previous billing, charges, tax, and total.

items.name: verify_all_billing_cycles_data
items.what_it_does: Verifies billing data for multiple billing cycles by iterating through expected data and selecting each cycle. For each cycle, opens dropdown, selects the option, waits for table update, and validates all fields.

items.name: validate_no_pens_print_history_page
items.what_it_does: Validates the print history page for printers without pens by checking which elements are visible and which are not. Verifies print history section and page title are visible, while detailed billing elements are not visible.

items.name: verify_plan_info
items.what_it_does: Verifies that the plan information on the print history page shows the expected price and page count. Extracts numbers from plan price text and validates both price and page count.
items.method_signature:
@staticmethod
def verify_plan_info(page, plan_value: str, plan_pages: str)

items.name: verify_current_total
items.what_it_does: Verifies that the current total value on the print history page matches the expected value. Extracts numbers from current total text and validates against expected value.

items.name: PrintHistoryHelper
items.what_it_does: Comprehensive helper module for print history page operations including verifying billing cycle periods, selecting billing cycle options, verifying billing table data, normalizing and validating currency values, verifying billing section details with expected values, validating all billing cycles data, validating FAQ links and navigation, validating no-pens print history page, downloading all invoices, verifying notifications, verifying plan information, and verifying current total values

items.name: OssEmulatorHelper
items.what_it_does: Helper module for OSS emulator operations including accessing the emulator, signing in, configuring printer details, selecting app types, starting flows, handling OOBE and POOBE flows, accepting agreements, declining HP Plus, handling dynamic security notices, value propositions, printer replacement, HP Plus activation, country selection, and verification of reminder checkboxes and trial text

items.name: access_page
items.what_it_does: Navigates to a specific page by clicking a navigation link with the provided page_name text. Uses an XPath selector to locate an anchor element with class 'nav-link' whose normalized text content exactly matches the page_name parameter. The XPath pattern is '//a[@class="nav-link" and normalize-space(text())="{page_name}"]'. The normalize-space function removes leading and trailing whitespace from the link text for more reliable matching. Once the link is located, clicks it to navigate to the target page. This method is commonly used to navigate through the application's main navigation menu.

items.name: wait_page_to_load
items.what_it_does: Waits for a specific page to fully load by checking for the presence of an h1 heading element containing the text 'Details for {page_title}'. Uses Playwright's wait_for_selector method with a dynamic selector constructed using an f-string that combines the page_title parameter into the selector pattern 'h1:has-text("Details for {page_title}")'. The method will wait up to the specified timeout duration (default 60 seconds) for the element to appear. If the element does not appear within the timeout, Playwright will throw a TimeoutError. This is commonly used after navigation to ensure the target page has fully loaded before proceeding with further interactions.

items.name: get_field_text_by_title
items.what_it_does: Retrieves the text content from a field within a card section identified by its title. First calls RABaseHelper.get_item_by_title() to get the card body element for the specified title. Then calls inner_text() on the field element to extract all visible text content including nested elements. Finally strips leading and trailing whitespace using strip() and returns the cleaned text string. This method is commonly used to extract data values from labeled fields on detail pages for validation in test assertions.

items.name: __getattr__
items.what_it_does: Implements dynamic attribute access to automatically convert selector strings into Playwright locator objects. When an attribute is accessed on the page object (e.g., page_obj.login_button), checks if the elements object has an attribute with that name using hasattr(self.elements, name). If found, retrieves the selector string using getattr(self.elements, name) and returns self.page.locator(locator_str) which creates and returns a Playwright Locator object for that selector. If the attribute name is not found in elements, raises an AttributeError with a descriptive message including the class name. This magic method enables page objects to access locators as simple attributes without manually creating locator objects, making page object code much cleaner and more readable.

items.name: RABaseHelper
items.what_it_does: Provides a comprehensive set of static utility methods for interacting with Resque job scheduler, managing background job execution, navigating application pages and menus, extracting data from page elements by title, accessing links within titled sections, validating boolean field values, and validating template data with date/time comparisons. This helper class encapsulates common patterns for Resque job orchestration and page element interaction that are reused across multiple test scenarios. All methods are static so no instantiation is required.

items.name: click_print_history
items.what_it_does: Clicks the Print and Payment History menu item in the side navigation to navigate to the print and payment history page. Calls _click_with_instant_ink_expansion with print_history_menu_link to handle mobile menu expansion and parent HP Instant Ink menu expansion if needed. This method provides a simple interface for test scripts to navigate to the history page.

items.name: faq_update_plan_link
items.what_it_does: Property method that returns a Playwright Locator object for the update plan link within the FAQ section. Calls self.page.locator() with the self.elements.update_plan_link selector to create a locator for the change plan link in the FAQ. Then calls .first on the locator to return only the first matching element.

items.name: plan_price_text
items.what_it_does: Property method that returns a Playwright Locator object for the plan price description text element. Calls self.page.locator() with the self.elements.plan_price_text selector to create a locator for the plan description element that displays plan pricing information. Then calls .first on the locator to return only the first matching element.

items.name: create_virtual_printer
items.what_it_does: Creates a virtual printer through the G2 simulator API with retry logic for each step. Returns PrinterData tuple with entity_id, model_number, device_uuid, cloud_id, postcard, and fingerprint.

items.name: create_and_claim_virtual_printer
items.what_it_does: Orchestrates complete printer creation and claiming flow. Combines get_org_aware_token(), create_virtual_printer(), and claim_virtual_printer().

items.name: create_and_claim_virtual_printer_and_add_address
items.what_it_does: Most complete printer setup including creation, claiming, and address addition. Prepares everything for enrollment.

items.name: claim_virtual_printer
items.what_it_does: Claims virtual printer to tenant account through UCDE API with retry logic.
items.method_signature: def claim_virtual_printer(org_token, tenant_id, model_number, device_uuid, postcard, fingerprint)

items.name: get_plans_data
items.what_it_does: Retrieves all Instant Ink plans data from Agena API for current country.

items.name: get_filtered_plan_data
items.what_it_does: Retrieves plans filtered by attribute value like page count.

items.name: subscription_data_from_gemini
items.what_it_does: Retrieves complete subscription data from Gemini API including state and free months.

items.name: get_subscription_state
items.what_it_does: Extracts subscription state from Gemini subscription data.

items.name: validate_subscription_state
items.what_it_does: Validates subscription reaches expected state within 60 seconds with polling and assertion.

items.name: send_rtp_devicestatus
items.what_it_does: Sends RTP device status telemetry to G2 simulator. Can be used to simulate printer activity and page printing by updating impression counts in the binary payload.

items.name: extract_numbers_from_text
items.what_it_does: Extracts all numeric values (integers and decimals) from text string using regex.

items.name: parse_flexible_date
items.what_it_does: Parses date strings in multiple formats into datetime objects.

items.name: extract_date_from_text
items.what_it_does: Extracts and parses date from text containing mixed content using regex patterns.

items.name: get_font_details
items.what_it_does: Extracts comprehensive font and text styling details from element using JavaScript evaluation of computed styles.

items.name: verify_font_details_property_value
items.what_it_does: Verifies CSS property has expected value with assertion.

items.name: is_element_actionable
items.what_it_does: Checks if element is actionable by verifying it's the topmost element at its center point.

items.name: get_org_aware_token
items.what_it_does: Obtains organization-aware user access token through multi-step token exchange process.

items.name: add_address_to_tenant
items.what_it_does: Adds shipping address to tenant account via Gemini comfe API.

items.name: create_ii_v2_account
items.what_it_does: Creates new Instant Ink v2 account through complete sign-up flow including email verification.

items.name: create_new_ii_v2_account
items.what_it_does: Enhanced account creation with support for password and OTP authentication flows.

items.name: login_ii_v2_account
items.what_it_does: Logs into existing Instant Ink v2 account with optional OOBE flow.

items.name: printer_colors
items.what_it_does: Returns ink colors list based on supply variant (IPH returns ['CMY', 'K'], IIC returns ['K', 'C', 'M', 'Y']).

items.name: test_flows_common
items.what_it_does: Comprehensive test flow helper module providing utilities for authentication, printer simulation, API calls, subscription management, and test data handling.

items.name: locale_map.json
items.what_it_does: Country/locale mapping with ISO codes, languages, regions, and payment gateway assignments for 38 countries.

items.name: offers.json
items.what_it_does: Offer configurations by country with GUIDs, identifiers, types (free/prepaid), and pricing.

items.name: payment_method_data.json
items.what_it_does: Payment method test credentials for credit cards, direct debit, PayPal, and Google Pay.

items.name: printer_profiles.json
items.constant_value: Printer SKU profiles by model and region
